Blood Disorders and Accessibility

blood_disorders_accessibility.Rmd

Blood disorders

site_file$blood_disorders

Site-level frequencies of inherited blood disorders that modify malaria risk. These are 2010 estimates.

Variables

sicklecell Sickle haemoglobin (HbS) allele frequency
gdp6 Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) deficiency allele frequency
hpc Haemoglobin C (HbC) allele frequency
duffy_negativity Duffy negativity phenotype frequency

Description

Allele and phenotype frequencies are taken from the Malaria Atlas Project blood disorder surfaces (2010 estimates). Duffy negativity is particularly relevant for Plasmodium vivax transmission, while HbS, HbC and G6PD deficiency influence Plasmodium falciparum disease risk.

Sources

🌍 | MAP

Accessibility

site_file$accessibility

Site-level estimates of travel time to healthcare and to the nearest city, summarised from the Malaria Atlas Project accessibility surfaces.

Variables

motor_travel_time_healthcare Motorised travel time to healthcare, minutes (2019 estimate)
walking_travel_time_healthcare Walking-only travel time to healthcare, minutes (2019 estimate)
city_travel_time Travel time to the nearest city, minutes (2015 estimate)

Description

These accessibility metrics are useful for interpreting access to treatment and for costing. Values are population-at-risk weighted summaries over each site.

Sources

🌍 | MAP

On this page
